summary: Turned the Wellframe Coach MCP server into a signed, installable .mcpb you can build in one command and drop into Claude Desktop. `npm run bundle` packs a lean production-only bundle, smoke-tests all 9 tools against real database states, and self-signs it. Extracted the two silent-failure-prone parsers into a tested module, and (in passing) fixed a repo-wide bug where the mcp-server tests corrupted the codeyam test gate.

changes.title: One-command bundle pipeline (pack → smoke → sign)
changes.description: New scripts under mcp-server/scripts/ build the .mcpb from a staged production-only tree (avoids sweeping in TypeScript, the mcpb CLI, and unused sql.js variants — ~50MB unpacked drops to ~10MB) and never mutate the dev tree. pack.sh compiles + stages + prunes + packs; the resulting bundle is ~3.3MB. Wired as npm scripts: pack, smoke, sign, bundle.

waysToTest.text: Read mcp-server/src/bundle.test.ts — covers the signature-block parser the pipeline depends on.
Run `cd mcp-server && npm install && npm run bundle`; a signed build/wellframe-coach.mcpb (~3.3MB) is produced.

changes.title: Packed-bundle smoke test across missing / empty / populated DBs
changes.description: smoke.mjs unpacks the actual packed .mcpb (not the dev tree) and drives all 9 MCP tools over stdio against three database states: a fresh install with no DB (must return a readable error, not crash), an empty DB (tools return empty cleanly), and a populated DB (real readiness/workouts/sleep output, proving sql.js WASM resolved inside the bundle). The fixture DB is built from the desktop app's own schema so it can never drift.

tests.name: desktopSchemaFrom › extracts the schema between the Rust string delimiters
tests.description: The schema the smoke fixture executes is pulled intact from the desktop Rust source.

tests.name: desktopSchemaFrom › stops at the first closing delimiter
tests.description: Non-greedy match: a later string literal is not swallowed into the schema.

tests.name: desktopSchemaFrom › throws a named error when the schema constant is absent
tests.description: A renamed SCHEMA constant fails loudly instead of building an empty fixture.

waysToTest.text: Run `cd mcp-server && npm run smoke`; every tool reports ok across all three DB states.

changes.title: Self-signing with a stable cert + independent signature verification
changes.description: sign.sh self-signs the bundle with a stable code-signing cert kept in .signing/ (gitignored; private key never committed) — deliberately NOT `mcpb sign --self-signed`, which ignores --cert/--key and writes the cert into node_modules/, changing the signing identity on every reinstall. Because mcpb 2.1.2's own `verify` reports every bundle as unsigned (node-forge's p7.verify throws 'not yet implemented'), verify-signature.mjs checks the real PKCS#7 with openssl instead — and fails if a single byte of the archive changed.

tests.name: extractSignatureBlock › splits a signed bundle into payload and signature
tests.description: Payload is returned byte-exact so the signature verifies against its digest.

tests.name: extractSignatureBlock › reports no signature for an unsigned bundle
tests.description: A fresh (unsigned) pack output reports no signature rather than throwing.

tests.name: extractSignatureBlock › rejects a block whose declared length does not reach the footer
tests.description: A truncated block is rejected instead of silently yielding a short signature.

tests.name: extractSignatureBlock › uses the last signature block when a bundle was signed twice
tests.description: Re-signing appends a block; the live (last) signature is the one read.

tests.name: extractSignatureBlock › ignores a footer that has no matching header
tests.description: A footer byte-sequence occurring by chance in the zip is not mistaken for a signature.

tests.name: extractSignatureBlock › handles an empty buffer
tests.description: The empty-input edge is guarded rather than throwing a range error.

waysToTest.text: Run `cd mcp-server && npm run sign` then `npm run verify-signature`; the signature validates over the exact bundle bytes.

changes.title: Fixed the mcp-server tests corrupting the codeyam test gate
changes.description: The mcp-server tests used node:test, whose stdout reporter corrupted the repo's `vitest run --reporter=json` output — the gate parsed 0 of 131 tests and the whole suite was invisible to codeyam. Converted both test files to vitest; the gate now reads all 146 tests green.

tests.name: fatigueIndex › reports when no signals exist
tests.description: A fresh install with no data explains why it cannot answer rather than fabricating a zero.

tests.name: generateTrainingPlan › is rest-led under very high fatigue
tests.description: Deeply fatigued athletes get a rest-skewed plan (safety-critical direction).

waysToTest.text: Run `cd mcp-server && npm test`; 15 tests pass and the JSON reporter is clean.

changes.title: Dev-only CSP relaxation so desktop scenarios can be captured
changes.description: The desktop app's index.html ships a strict `default-src 'self'` CSP that blocked codeyam's capture harness for every desktop scenario. A dev-only Vite plugin (apply: 'serve') relaxes it to allow inline scripts on the dev server only — the built dist and the production Tauri app (governed by tauri.conf.json) are unchanged. This unblocked capturing the App entry-point scenario.

waysToTest.text: View the app-primed-dashboard scenario — the App router rendered at / with the Primed dashboard fixture.
curl the dev server root; the served CSP includes script-src 'unsafe-inline', while dist/index.html stays strict.

changes.title: Brought mcp-server into codeyam alignment (thin the fatigue gatherer)
changes.description: Registered the mcp-server's pre-existing functions in the glossary and, to give the DB-gathering fatigueInputs honest coverage instead of a hand-waved exemption, extracted its two bits of real logic into pure, unit-tested analysis helpers: toCheckinRatings (snake_case DB rows → ratings shape) and parseMetricNumber (leading number out of a metric string, zero→null). fatigueInputs is now a thin boundary that delegates to them. Also classified the retired Next.js app/api route handlers as framework handlers via stack.json so they no longer read as unregistered entities.

tests.name: toCheckinRatings › remaps snake_case DB rows to the ratings shape
tests.description: sleep_quality is renamed to sleepQuality so the fatigue math sees the value.

tests.name: toCheckinRatings › preserves null fields
tests.description: Null ratings carry through so a missing rating isn't treated as zero.

tests.name: parseMetricNumber › extracts the numeric value from a metric string
tests.description: Pulls the leading number out of display strings like '7.4 h' and '1,240'.

tests.name: parseMetricNumber › returns null for null, undefined, or non-numeric input
tests.description: Absent or non-numeric input is 'no signal', returned as null rather than NaN.

tests.name: parseMetricNumber › treats a zero value as null
tests.description: A parsed 0 collapses to null, matching the original inline behavior.

waysToTest.text: Run `cd mcp-server && npm test` then `npm run smoke`; the refactor is behavior-preserving and all tools still return correct output.
